description: Versioned prompt-injection / memory-poisoning attack corpus for Rekoll's firewall (ADR-0013, ADR-0020). Two CI gates run over it (tests/test_attack_corpus.py): a CONTAINMENT gate (must be 100% — no untrusted attack ever reaches the recall envelope's instruction channel) and a DETECTION/ASR gate (quarantine rate at ingest may only rise; ASR may only fall). `detected` records whether the ingest tripwire is EXPECTED to quarantine the payload today; entries with `detected:false` are contained by the structural defense, not the marker list — that is by design (DESIGN §6, 'the regex screen is a tripwire, not the wall').
notes: Payloads are inert strings, not executable. This file is written with ensure_ascii=True, so EVERY non-ASCII codepoint (invisible chars, bidi controls, homoglyphs, and all multilingual text) is a \uXXXX escape — the file stays fully reviewable and GitHub will not banner it for hidden bidi Unicode. Key escapes: \u200b=ZWSP, \u00ad=soft hyphen, \u202e=RLO, \u0456=Cyrillic 'i' homoglyph.
